79	def _enable_file_logging(log_file_path: str) -> None:
80	    """Attach file logger so every run persists diagnostics to disk."""
81	    if not log_file_path:
82	        return
83	
84	    root_logger = logging.getLogger()
85	    abs_path = os.path.abspath(log_file_path)
86	
87	    # Avoid duplicate handlers if called more than once in the same process.
88	    for handler in root_logger.handlers:
89	        if isinstance(handler, logging.FileHandler) and getattr(handler, "baseFilename", None) == abs_path:
90	            return
91	
92	    file_handler = logging.FileHandler(abs_path, mode="a", encoding="utf-8")
93	    file_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
94	    file_handler.setFormatter(logging.Formatter("%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
95	    root_logger.addHandler(file_handler)
96	    log.info(f"File logging enabled: {abs_path}")

145	def _unzip(file_path: str, extract_to: str) -> bool:
146	    """Extract a zip file. Returns True on success."""
147	    try:
148	        with zipfile.ZipFile(file_path, 'r') as zip_ref:
149	            zip_ref.extractall(extract_to)
150	        log.debug(f"Extracted ZIP: {file_path}")
151	        return True
152	    except Exception as e:
153	        log.error(f"Error extracting ZIP {file_path}: {e}")
154	        shutil.rmtree(extract_to, ignore_errors=True)
155	        return False

158	def _extract_path(path_name: str, s3_mode: bool = False) -> None:
159	    """
160	    Recursively walk a directory. Extract ZIP files in place.
161	
162	    Args:
163	        path_name: Root directory to walk.
164	        s3_mode: If True, extract-only (don't re-zip). If False, re-zip after processing.
165	    """
166	    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(path_name):
167	        for file in files:
168	            filepath = os.path.join(root, file)
169	
170	            if file.endswith(('.zip', '.ZIP')):
171	                temp_dir = os.path.join(root, f"zip_{os.path.splitext(file)[0]}")
172	                os.makedirs(temp_dir, exist_ok=True)
173	
174	                if not _unzip(filepath, temp_dir):
175	                    continue
176	
177	                try:
178	                    _extract_path(temp_dir, s3_mode=s3_mode)
179	
180	                    if s3_mode:
181	                        os.remove(filepath)
182	                        log.debug(f"Removed ZIP {filepath} (S3 mode: extract-only)")
183	                    else:
184	                        os.remove(filepath)
185	                        shutil.make_archive(os.path.splitext(filepath)[0], 'zip', temp_dir)
186	                        log.debug(f"Re-zipped {filepath} (local mode)")
187	
188	                    shutil.rmtree(temp_dir, ignore_errors=True)
189	                except Exception as e:
190	                    log.error(f"Error processing ZIP {filepath}: {e}")
191	                    shutil.rmtree(temp_dir, ignore_errors=True)

200	def _to_decimal(v):
201	    """Convert float to Decimal for DynamoDB; guard NaN/Inf."""
202	    if isinstance(v, float):
203	        if math.isnan(v) or math.isinf(v):
204	            return Decimal(0)
205	        return Decimal(str(v))
206	    return v

209	def _get_skip_paths(table) -> set:
210	    """Return file paths to skip: COMPLETED or ERROR with retries exhausted.
211	
212	    Uses GSI1 (GSI1PK = STATUS#{status}) instead of a full table scan —
213	    O(result set) not O(table size). Requires GSI1 with ProjectionType=ALL.
214	    """
215	    skip = set()
216	
217	    # COMPLETED — skip unconditionally
218	    kwargs: dict = dict(
219	        IndexName="GSI1",
220	        KeyConditionExpression=Key("GSI1PK").eq("STATUS#COMPLETED"),
221	        ProjectionExpression="file_path",
222	    )
223	    while True:
224	        resp = table.query(**kwargs)
225	        for item in resp.get("Items", []):
226	            if "file_path" in item:
227	                skip.add(item["file_path"])
228	        if "LastEvaluatedKey" not in resp:
229	            break
230	        kwargs["ExclusiveStartKey"] = resp["LastEvaluatedKey"]
231	
232	    # ERROR — skip only if retries exhausted
233	    kwargs = dict(
234	        IndexName="GSI1",
235	        KeyConditionExpression=Key("GSI1PK").eq("STATUS#ERROR"),
236	        ProjectionExpression="file_path, retryAttempts",
237	    )
238	    while True:
239	        resp = table.query(**kwargs)
240	        for item in resp.get("Items", []):
241	            if int(item.get("retryAttempts", 0)) >= MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS:
242	                if "file_path" in item:
243	                    skip.add(item["file_path"])
244	        if "LastEvaluatedKey" not in resp:
245	            break
246	        kwargs["ExclusiveStartKey"] = resp["LastEvaluatedKey"]
247	
248	    return skip

273	def _sanitize_report_for_dynamo(report: dict) -> dict:
274	    """Convert all floats in a report dict to Decimal for DynamoDB compatibility."""
275	    sanitized = {}
276	    for k, v in report.items():
277	        if isinstance(v, float):
278	            sanitized[k] = _to_decimal(v)
279	        elif isinstance(v, dict):
280	            sanitized[k] = _sanitize_report_for_dynamo(v)
281	        elif isinstance(v, list):
282	            sanitized[k] = [
283	                _sanitize_report_for_dynamo(i) if isinstance(i, dict)
284	                else _to_decimal(i) if isinstance(i, float)
285	                else i
286	                for i in v
287	            ]
288	        elif isinstance(v, bool):
289	            sanitized[k] = v
290	        else:
291	            sanitized[k] = v
292	    return sanitized

501	def _process_image(image_path: str) -> dict:
502	    """
503	    Run the full masking pipeline on one image via core.pipeline.
504	
505	    All masking logic lives in core/pipeline.py (single source of truth).
506	    This wrapper handles only file I/O and GPU memory cleanup.
507	    """
508	    try:
509	        image = cv2.imread(image_path, cv2.IMREAD_COLOR)
510	        if image is None:
511	            raise ValueError(f"Cannot read image: {image_path}")
512	        if image.ndim == 2:
513	            image = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_GRAY2BGR)
514	
515	        masked_image, report = process_image(image, skip_keywords_enabled=True)
516	        if not cv2.imwrite(image_path, masked_image):
517	            raise IOError(f"cv2.imwrite failed for {image_path}")
518	        return report
519	
520	    finally:
521	        if 'image' in locals():
522	            del image
523	        if 'masked_image' in locals():
524	            del masked_image
525	        gc.collect()

528	def _process_pdf(pdf_path: str, dest_path: str) -> dict:
529	    """Convert PDF → images → mask each → rebuild PDF. Returns per-page report."""
530	    from pdf2image import pdfinfo_from_path
531	
532	    # Check page count before loading (fast, no image decode)
533	    try:
534	        info = pdfinfo_from_path(pdf_path)
535	        total_pages = info.get("Pages", 0)
536	    except Exception as e:
537	        log.warning(f"pdfinfo failed for {pdf_path}: {e} — page limit check skipped")
538	        total_pages = 0  # proceed and let convert_from_path handle it
539	
540	    if total_pages > MAX_PDF_PAGES:
541	        raise ValueError(f"PDF has {total_pages} pages, limit is {MAX_PDF_PAGES}")
542	
543	    page_reports = {}
544	    CHUNK_SIZE = PDF_CHUNK_SIZE
545	
546	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
547	        image_paths = []
548	        page_idx = 0
549	
550	        # Process in chunks to avoid loading all pages into memory at once
551	        start = 1
552	        end = total_pages if total_pages > 0 else 9999  # fallback if pdfinfo failed
553	
554	        while start <= end:
555	            chunk_end = min(start + CHUNK_SIZE - 1, end)
556	            try:
557	                pages = convert_from_path(
558	                    pdf_path, dpi=200, first_page=start, last_page=chunk_end
559	                )
560	            except Exception as e:
561	                if start == 1 and total_pages == 0:
562	                    raise  # first chunk failed, re-raise
563	                log.warning(f"PDF chunk {start}-{chunk_end} failed: {e}")
564	                break
565	
566	            if not pages:
567	                break
568	
569	            for page in pages:
570	                img_path = os.path.join(tmp, f"p{page_idx:04d}.jpg")
571	                page.convert('RGB').save(img_path, "JPEG")  # Force RGB — YOLO requires 3 channels
572	                del page
573	
574	                try:
575	                    report = _process_image(img_path)
576	                except Exception as e:
577	                    log.warning(f"Page {page_idx + 1} error: {e}")
578	                    report = {"error": str(e)}
579	                    # Replace with blank image — never include unmasked PII in output PDF
580	                    try:
581	                        import numpy as np
582	                        blank = np.zeros((2480, 1754, 3), dtype=np.uint8)  # A4 at 200dpi
583	                        cv2.imwrite(img_path, blank)
584	                    except Exception as blank_err:
585	                        # Blank write failed (disk full / permissions). Fallback: include
586	                        # original page so the PDF is complete. Logged clearly for audit.
587	                        log.error(
588	                            f"Page {page_idx + 1}: masking failed ({e}) AND blank replacement "
589	                            f"failed ({blank_err}) — original unmasked page included in output PDF."
590	                        )
591	                        page_reports[str(page_idx + 1)] = {

748	    Raises the last exception if all attempts fail.

750	    delay = 1
751	    last_err = None
752	    for attempt in range(attempts):
753	        try:
754	            return fn(*args, **kwargs)
755	        except Exception as e:
756	            last_err = e
757	            if attempt < attempts - 1:
758	                log.warning(f"DynamoDB call failed (attempt {attempt+1}/{attempts}): {e} — retrying in {delay}s")
759	                time.sleep(delay)
760	                delay *= 2
761	    raise last_err

### Local variable vs DB — answer

**Use DynamoDB.** Here's why:

| Factor | Local variable | DynamoDB |
|---|---|---|
| Crash at file 150 of 300 | Lose all state, re-download 0-149 | Resume from 151 |
| "Why was this file skipped?" | Lost after process exits | Permanent audit trail |
| Multi-run resumability | Impossible | Free via existing skip_paths |

The only argument for local var is simplicity — but you already have the DB infrastructure, so the marginal cost is just a few extra writes.

## 1. **New fields in DynamoDB record schema** (core/db/database.py)

Add these to `build_default_record()`:

```python
{
    "PK": "DOC#s3://...",
    "SK": "2026-05-15T...",
    "status": "PENDING",                    # existing: PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED/ERROR
    "GSI1PK": "STATUS#PENDING",             # existing
    
    # ── NEW: Download state ──
    "download_status": "QUEUED",            # QUEUED | DOWNLOADED | DOWNLOAD_FAILED | DOWNLOAD_SKIPPED
    "download_timestamp": "2026-05-15T...", # when download was attempted
    "download_error": None,                 # if DOWNLOAD_FAILED: the exception message
    "download_skip_reason": None,           # if DOWNLOAD_SKIPPED: "size_exceeded" | "keyword_match" | "password_protected"
    "download_retry_count": 0,              # how many times we tried to download this file
    "local_staging_path": None,             # path in /tmp/ahfl_staging/{month}/ where file was downloaded
}
```

---

## 2. **New helper functions** (batch.py)

Add these DB update functions:

```python
def _update_download_queued(table, pk: str, sk: str) -> None:
    """Mark file as QUEUED for download (added to batch list)."""
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    table.update_item(
        Key={"PK": pk, "SK": sk},
        UpdateExpression="SET download_status = :ds, download_timestamp = :ts, download_retry_count = :zero",
        ExpressionAttributeValues={
            ":ds": "QUEUED",
            ":ts": now,
            ":zero": 0,
        },
    )

def _update_download_successful(table, pk: str, sk: str, local_path: str) -> None:
    """Mark file as DOWNLOADED (in staging dir, ready to process)."""
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    table.update_item(
        Key={"PK": pk, "SK": sk},
        UpdateExpression="SET download_status = :ds, download_timestamp = :ts, local_staging_path = :lp",
        ExpressionAttributeValues={
            ":ds": "DOWNLOADED",
            ":ts": now,
            ":lp": local_path,
        },
    )

def _update_download_failed(table, pk: str, sk: str, error_msg: str) -> None:
    """Mark file as DOWNLOAD_FAILED with error reason."""
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    table.update_item(
        Key={"PK": pk, "SK": sk},
        UpdateExpression=(
            "SET download_status = :ds, download_timestamp = :ts, "
            "download_error = :err ADD download_retry_count :one"
        ),
        ExpressionAttributeValues={
            ":ds": "DOWNLOAD_FAILED",
            ":ts": now,
            ":err": error_msg[:500],
            ":one": 1,
        },
    )

def _update_download_skipped(table, pk: str, sk: str, skip_reason: str) -> None:
    """Mark file as DOWNLOAD_SKIPPED with reason (size, keyword, etc.)."""
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    table.update_item(
        Key={"PK": pk, "SK": sk},
        UpdateExpression="SET download_status = :ds, download_timestamp = :ts, download_skip_reason = :reason",
        ExpressionAttributeValues={
            ":ds": "DOWNLOAD_SKIPPED",
            ":ts": now,
            ":reason": skip_reason,
        },
    )
```

---

Current:
```python
def _write_pending(table, pk: str, sk: str, file_path: str, s3_key: str = "") -> None:
    record = build_default_record(pk, sk, file_path, s3_key=s3_key)

```

Update:
```python
def _write_pending(table, pk: str, sk: str, file_path: str, s3_key: str = "") -> None:
    record = build_default_record(pk, sk, file_path, s3_key=s3_key)
    record["id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
    record["download_status"] = "QUEUED"  # ← NEW
    record["download_retry_count"] = 0     # ← NEW
    table.put_item(Item=record)
```

---

## 4. **Modify `_get_skip_paths()` to exclude QUEUED/DOWNLOADING files**

Current logic: skip COMPLETED + ERROR-exhausted files.

New logic:
```python
def _get_skip_paths(table) -> set:
    """Return file paths to skip: COMPLETED or ERROR with retries exhausted.
    
    Does NOT skip:
    - QUEUED: needs download attempt
    - DOWNLOADED: ready for processing
    - DOWNLOAD_FAILED: needs retry
    """
    skip = set()
    
    # COMPLETED — skip unconditionally
    kwargs = dict(
        IndexName="GSI1",
        KeyConditionExpression=Key("GSI1PK").eq("STATUS#COMPLETED"),
        ProjectionExpression="file_path",
    )
    while True:
        resp = table.query(**kwargs)
        for item in resp.get("Items", []):
            if "file_path" in item:
                skip.add(item["file_path"])
        if "LastEvaluatedKey" not in resp:
            break
        kwargs["ExclusiveStartKey"] = resp["LastEvaluatedKey"]
    
    # ERROR — skip only if retries exhausted
    kwargs = dict(
        IndexName="GSI1",
        KeyConditionExpression=Key("GSI1PK").eq("STATUS#ERROR"),
        ProjectionExpression="file_path, retryAttempts",
    )
    while True:
        resp = table.query(**kwargs)
        for item in resp.get("Items", []):
            if int(item.get("retryAttempts", 0)) >= MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS:
                if "file_path" in item:
                    skip.add(item["file_path"])
        if "LastEvaluatedKey" not in resp:
            break
        kwargs["ExclusiveStartKey"] = resp["LastEvaluatedKey"]
    
    return skip
```

No change here — logic stays the same. The download_status is independent.

---

## 5. **Migration for existing records** (optional, can be done at app startup)

For records already in the table without `download_status`, run a scan + update to add defaults:

```python
def _migrate_download_status(table) -> None:
    """Add download_status to old records. Safe to run multiple times."""
    scan_kwargs = dict(
        FilterExpression=Attr("download_status").not_exists(),
        ProjectionExpression="PK, SK, status",
    )
    migrated = 0
    while True:
        response = table.scan(**scan_kwargs)
        for item in response.get("Items", []):
            # Set based on current status
            status = item.get("status", "PENDING")
            if status == "COMPLETED":
                ds = "DOWNLOADED"  # was already processed
            elif status == "ERROR":
                ds = "DOWNLOAD_FAILED"  # download or processing failed
            else:
                ds = "QUEUED"  # default for PENDING or unknown
            
            table.update_item(
                Key={"PK": item["PK"], "SK": item["SK"]},
                UpdateExpression="SET download_status = :ds, download_retry_count = :zero",
                ExpressionAttributeValues={":ds": ds, ":zero": 0},
            )
            migrated += 1
        
        if "LastEvaluatedKey" not in response:
            break
        scan_kwargs["ExclusiveStartKey"] = response["LastEvaluatedKey"]
    
    log.info(f"Migration: added download_status to {migrated} records")
```

---

## Summary of DB schema changes

| Field | Type | Values | Purpose |
|-------|------|--------|---------|
| `download_status` | String | QUEUED \| DOWNLOADED \| DOWNLOAD_FAILED \| DOWNLOAD_SKIPPED | Track download phase state |
| `download_timestamp` | ISO8601 | datetime | When download was last attempted |
| `download_error` | String | error message (500 chars max) | Root cause if DOWNLOAD_FAILED |
| `download_skip_reason` | String | "size_exceeded" \| "keyword_match" \| ... | Why file was skipped |
| `download_retry_count` | Number | 0, 1, 2, ... | How many download attempts so far |
| `local_staging_path` | String | "/tmp/ahfl_staging/{month}/..." | Where file sits if DOWNLOADED |

These are **independent of** the existing `status` field (PENDING/PROCESSING/COMPLETED/ERROR). They track the download phase specifically.
